narrative: Today's feed leans heavily on landscape-ecology and methods papers alongside a strong climate showing led by dam-management effects on Yellowstone cutthroat trout productivity and coastal nursery phenology in threatened Chinook salmon. In movement and behaviour, Tasmanian devils and spotted-tailed quolls turned out to be secondary dispersers of mycorrhizal fungal spores via scat, while a free-ranging lizard showed active behavioural regulation of UV exposure independent of thermoregulation. Trophic and herbivory work spanned a new zoogeochemistry framework linking animal activity to biogeochemical cycling, UV-B/silicon interactions in cereal anti-herbivore defence, and vertebrate herbivory history shaping ontogenetic defence shifts on oceanic islands. Invasion ecology saw litter allelochemicals implicated in invasional meltdown among alien plants and a global quantification of freshwater fish invasion impacts. Landscape and conservation papers ranged from clearcuts as potential ecological traps for caribou to a re-examination of the decades-old SLOSS debate via a new REBSEN model for fragmented tropical landscapes, plus a three-decade retrospective on IUCN Red List assessments for sharks, rays and chimaeras. On methods, a large evidence-synthesis paper tracked a fifty-year shift in conservation study designs toward more robust BACI and randomised approaches, while several Landscape Ecology papers debuted remote-sensing and machine-learning pipelines for mapping dry stone walls, forest biomass and connectivity.

articles.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
articles.digest: Random forest model combining inventory records, canopy height and climate maps boreal forest age at continental scale, revealing fire- and logging-driven bimodal age structure.

articles.title: Pinus yunnanensis var. pygmaea individuals are more ready to embrace wildfire in more fire‐prone areas

articles.title: Industrialization Drives a Late‐19th‐Century Pulse in Appalachian Fire Activity
articles.digest: Nineteenth-century industrialization triggered a statistically extreme pulse in Appalachian fire activity, reorganizing regional fire regimes beyond historical variability.

methods_radar.title: A New Framework to Empower Ecosystem Assessment Through the Integration of eDNA Inventories, Graph Theory and Niche Modelling
methods_radar.note: New framework combining eDNA inventories, graphlet-based network analysis and niche modelling for scalable ecosystem assessment.

methods_radar.title: Testing the Influence of Internal Stem Decay and Topography on Biomass and Carbon Stock Estimates in Mongolia’s Boreal Forest
methods_radar.note: New regression approach integrates stem decay and slope inclination into stand aboveground biomass estimates, refining allometric methods.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Multi-source data and a machine learning algorithm estimate forest soil organic carbon density across dry tropical valleys.

methods_radar.title: Defaunation Maps for Tropical Mammals and Birds
methods_radar.note: Novel modelling framework links hunting-pressure drivers to wildlife population impacts, producing pantropical defaunation maps for mammals and birds.

methods_radar.title: Automated mapping of dry stone walls in terraced landscapes using airborne laser scanning data
methods_radar.note: New automated pipeline compares Random Forest and deep-learning segmentation of airborne laser scanning DTMs to map dry stone walls.

methods_radar.title: Modelling density surfaces from integrated passive acoustic and point count data streams
methods_radar.note: New model integrates passive acoustic monitoring with point-count distance sampling to produce absolute density surfaces.

methods_radar.title: BIMr2: Inference of migration rates and their drivers from multilocus genotypes
methods_radar.note: BIMr2 improves on BIMr for inferring migration rates and environmental drivers from multilocus genotype data, scaling to more populations.

methods_radar.title: ForestForTrees: An R package to infer forest structure from remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: ForestForTrees R package infers forest size-abundance structure from remote sensing without requiring advanced Bayesian programming skills.

methods_radar.title: Pan‐boreal mapping of forest age and implications for conservation
methods_radar.note: Random forest model combining inventory, canopy height and climate data produces continental-scale boreal forest age maps.

methods_radar.title: Comparative species distribution model framework for marine conservation and its application to loggerhead turtles in the Mediterranean
methods_radar.note: Benchmarking workflow compares species distribution model performance for wide-ranging marine species, tested on Mediterranean loggerhead turtles.

methods_radar.title: Temporal and spatial trends in study designs used to understand what works in conservation
methods_radar.note: Bayesian multinomial regression on 8,500+ studies tracks the conservation evidence base's shift toward more rigorous BACI and randomised designs.

methods_radar.title: Discovering the unseen: A performance comparison of taxonomic classification methods for unknown DNA barcodes
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks taxonomic classification methods for unidentified DNA barcodes against incomplete reference databases.

methods_radar.title: Addressing false negatives and positives in eDNA studies
methods_radar.note: Reviews reporting of false positive/negative detection errors in eDNA studies to improve methodological transparency.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Dynamic occupancy models applied to six years of wolf presence data assess colonisation and persistence drivers in the Alps.

methods_radar.title: Species traits mediate the abundant‐center patterns in ground‐dwelling mammal and bird species in China
methods_radar.note: Camera-trap relative abundance indices for 81 species test the abundant-center hypothesis and trait mediators across China.

methods_radar.title: Ground‐to‐canopy monitoring reveals hidden ecological patterns in Congo Basin mammals
methods_radar.note: Standardised vertically-stratified camera trapping extends biodiversity monitoring from ground to canopy in Congo Basin forests.

methods_radar.title: Is There Mush-room for Bettongs on Dirk Hartog Island? Environmental DNA Assessment of Food Resource Availability for Fauna Translocations
methods_radar.note: eDNA metabarcoding characterises fungal food resources ahead of a threatened marsupial translocation to Dirk Hartog Island.

methods_radar.title: Topography is a Major Determinant of Forest–Savanna Distributions in Mosaic Landscapes in Central Africa
methods_radar.note: Machine learning models trained on remotely sensed tree cover and topographic variables predict forest-savanna mosaic states in Central Africa.

methods_radar.title: From Deficiency to Enrichment: Diverging Soil Phosphorus Trajectories Across China's Croplands (1980–2018)
methods_radar.note: Harmonised national soil surveys and a machine-learning modelling framework reconstruct four decades of cropland phosphorus trajectories.

methods_radar.title: Predicting Coral Offspring Survival From Reef Thermal Histories in Ningaloo World Heritage Reef
methods_radar.note: Boosted regression tree analysis links satellite sea-surface temperature histories to coral larval survival across 51 selectively bred families.

methods_radar.title: Strategic dam management can offset climate‐driven declines in fish production
methods_radar.note: Hierarchical Bayesian stock-recruitment model quantifies six decades of climate and dam-flow effects on trout productivity.

methods_radar.title: Enhancing rule-based potential connectivity models for African savanna elephants (Loxodonta africana) using remote sensing in Binga District, Zimbabwe
methods_radar.note: Rule-based resistance connectivity model for savanna elephants improved with detailed remote-sensing land-cover classification versus global LULC maps.

methods_radar.title: Not single large, not several small: the Requirement Estimation Based on Species Ecological Needs (REBSEN) is the best conceptual model to define conservation strategies in severely fragmented landscapes
methods_radar.note: Applies satellite imagery, land-use history and field surveys within a guild-based classification to test the SLOSS framework.

methods_radar.title: Effects of urban vegetation on the surface urban heat island: a UAV approach with a focus on private gardens
methods_radar.note: UAV multispectral imagery and Random Forest classification quantify urban green infrastructure cooling effects during a heatwave.

methods_radar.title: Spatiotemporal dynamics and cross-boundary connectivity of an ecological network across a fragmented landscape
methods_radar.note: Landsat time series, Random Forest classification and graph-based connectivity indices applied via Google Earth Engine to map fragmentation.

methods_radar.title: Multiscale species distribution models reveal seascape niche partitioning and overlap in native and alien fishes
methods_radar.note: Multi-scale species distribution models on a 1 m bathymetric seascape assess niche overlap between invasive and native reef fish.

methods_radar.title: A multi-satellite comparison of forest growth between karst and adjacent non-karst nature reserves
methods_radar.note: Multisource satellite vegetation indices (EVI, LAI, NIRv) compare forest growth dynamics between karst and non-karst nature reserves.

methods_radar.title: Farmland bird diversity requires heterogeneity between and within habitats
methods_radar.note: Passive acoustic monitoring with BirdNET across 224 recorders surveys farmland bird diversity in mosaic agricultural landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Are forestry clearcuts ecological traps for caribou?
methods_radar.note: Step-selection functions on GPS-collared caribou combined with camera-trap predator detections assess clearcuts as ecological traps.

methods_radar.title: Maximizing eDNA detections of rare marine organisms: A case study of a delphinid species
methods_radar.note: Evaluates eDNA sampling and processing methods to optimise detection of a rare delphinid species.

methods_radar.title: Quaternary Ice Ages Shaped Protists Phylogeography: The Case of Arcellinida in the Iberian Peninsula
methods_radar.note: Metabarcoding of testate amoebae tests the genetic-legacy-of-Quaternary paradigm in microbial eukaryotes across the Iberian Peninsula.

methods_radar.title: Resolving Emergent Patterns in Community Genetics With Environmental DNA
methods_radar.note: Environmental DNA approach simultaneously characterises community composition and genetic variation, replacing traditional individual-sampling genetics methods.

methods_radar.title: Tropical forest restoration can offset water flux losses from deforestation but not everywhere
methods_radar.note: Satellite observations of evapotranspiration and precipitation quantify asymmetric water-flux recovery from tropical forest restoration versus deforestation losses.

methods_radar.title: Asymmetric transferability of trait–environment relationships between native and exotic plant species in Singaporean forests differing in land‐use history
methods_radar.note: Joint species distribution models test whether trait-environment relationships transfer from native to exotic plant species.
